title: Ctenitopsis sasakii
taxonomy: Ctenitopsis sasakii is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. This family comprises over 3,000 species of ferns that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Ctenitopsis sasakii is a species of fern that is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Ctenitopsis sasakii is further detailed at the genus and species levels, but it is generally categorized within the kingdom Plantae and the division Pteridophyta. At the higher taxonomic levels, it is classified within the kingdom Plantae and the clade Streptophyta.

intro: Ctenitopsis sasakii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in folk medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitopsis sasakii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitopsis sasakii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and skin disorders.
